I continue to have a very disappointing experience with Carlo Fusco. From the start, communication has been poor—I must follow up multiple times just to get basic updates on my case. It is now Feb 2025 and I still have not heard back from him in over 6 weeks, even after emailing Carlo on 3 separate occasions during January alone. This is an ongoing issue with Carlo which I communicated several times but nothing has improved. When I finally get responses, they are vague and lack clear guidance or progress.
Additionally, I feel that my case is not being given the attention it deserves. Deadlines continue to be missed, and there seems to be little effort in preparing a strong strategy.  For example, in January 2024, we discussed having a private investigator serve papers to a defendant in my case, and it was not until December 2024 that Carlo asked me to verify the defendent 's social media accounts that the private investigator found.

Unfortunately, this lack of professionalism and diligence continues to cost me both time and money. I expected much better from a legal professional and would not recommend this firm to anyone seeking reliable representation.

As a small business owner it is critical to have a legal team in place ready for when things come up!! I’m extremely disappointed in how my case was handled — not just by attorney Ramy Joudeh, but by the entire law firm that was supposed to take care of me after he left. I was hit by a car and went to them for help, thinking I’d be supported through the process. Instead, I felt pressured to settle for far less than what my case was actually worth. Ramy kept pushing me to accept a low offer and discouraged me from going to court, even when I voiced my concerns. It felt like he just wanted to close the case quickly, not fight for what I truly deserved.

What’s worse is that after Ramy Joudeh left the firm, I expected the law office to step in, do the right thing, and guide me properly through the rest of the process. But that never happened. No one really took over with care or transparency. I was left in the dark, and in the end, I received a very small settlement — and then got hit with made-up fees I was never clearly told about. It honestly felt like they just gave up on me and cashed out.

I trusted this law firm to advocate for me when I needed it most. Instead, I walked away feeling abandoned and taken advantage of. I would not recommend them to anyone looking for real legal help or a team that actually has your back.

I couldn’t have asked for better attorneys than Chopra and Nocerino. They fought hard for me after my car accident from start to finish. They guided me through every step, always kept me informed, and fought hard to get the best outcome. Professional, compassionate, and truly had my best interests at heart. I’m so grateful for their support—they made the whole process smooth and stress-free. Highly recommend!

reviews.text:
I can't say enough great things about Chopra & Nocerino! They fought tirelessly for me and secured a settlement far beyond what I ever expected. What truly impressed me was their keen attention to detail—while my doctors initially treated two injuries as separate, the firm was able to prove they were connected, significantly increasing my settlement.

Their communication was outstanding throughout the entire process, always keeping me informed and addressing every concern. Alex Nocerino truly fought for me every step of the way, and Todd Arbesfeld made my repeated depositions much easier, guiding me through each one and helping me overcome my "stage fright." Sam and Alex, both being parents themselves, understood the anxiety and fear I had for my daughter and promised they would do what no other law firm would be able to do for her. They fought diligently for her. They WON for her.
Sameer Chopra and Alex Nocerino not only got a settlement that would ensure my daughters future and put ease on obstacles ahead, they also helped a single Mother regain a life with her daughter. I can exhale knowing she’s going to be fine. Writing this review has been very emotional. The amount of support this law firm has given us couldn’t amount in words to how grateful I am.
From the bottom of my heart, THANK YOU.

A++ I had to leave a review being that Luigi has now saved my butt twice. A few years back I was stopped wrongfully for being on the phone and was ticketed for that and several other things in Suffolk county. I called multiple law offices and most wanted an absurd amount of money with no promise or thought of even getting the points reduced. Luigi explained to me my options and right away it felt like I was in good hands.  I went to court with Luigi and I was in and out in less then 20 minutes. He got everything cleared from my license. The fine I ended up having to pay was near nothing.

Fast forward two years to this past month. I got pulled over again and received similar tickets. Luigi was my first thought and I was worried he wouldn’t take the case because this time it was in Nassau county. Thankfully I still had the number saved. I gave him a call, and he assured me he could handle it. This time I did not have to appear in court. Luigi went for me and same thing, all points cleared and a tiny fee. I will always recommend this office.  Thank you Luigi!

I recently had the opportunity to work with Joseph Armao and Blanca Solorzano and their team at The Licatesi Law Group regarding a personal injury matter, and I am incredibly grateful for the professionalism, compassion, and dedication they showed throughout the process.

The entire staff was equally impressive.  Blanca was consistently responsive and kept me informed at every step. Communication was always prompt and respectful, and I was never left wondering about the status of my case.  Notably, she was very patient with answering any of my questions.   Joseph fought hard to ensure that I received fair compensation for my injuries, and their efforts led to a result that far exceeded my expectations.
I highly recommend Joseph Armao and the team at The Licatesi Law Group to anyone in need of a personal injury attorney. They are not only excellent legal professionals but also kind and supportive people who truly advocate for their clients.
Thank you again for your outstanding work and dedication!
reviews.ownerResponse:

We're thrilled that the outcome exceeded your expectations, and we wish you continued healing and peace of mind as you move forward. Should you ever need anything in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out -we’re always here to help.

Resting & Murphy LLP is the best law firm I have worked with. They are very well, diverse with encouragement, support, and education. They work one on one with you and help me make the greatest decision with my cases. Thank you for all you have done for me.

I’d never dealt with a personal injury case before and hope this is my last, but if I need an attorney again, Patrick and his team at Restivo and Murphy will be my only call.

Vanessa went above and beyond, even scheduling my doctor’s appointments. Getting into a neurologist was impossible until she stepped in, finding convenient doctors and setting up PT. Patrick explained every step of the process clearly, so I always knew what was happening and what I needed to do (mostly just attend appointments). If Patrick was unavailable, Joseph was fully informed and handled everything professionally.

Communication was outstanding throughout.I’d tell any family member or friend not to waste time looking elsewhere—Restivo and Murphy are the best in the business.

Lonika is one of the most caring, thorough, and professional attorneys I've ever met. She always made time for me, personally answered my calls, and explained everything so I never felt unsure. She and her team stood by me every step of the way. I truly thank you from the bottom of my heart!"
RESTIVO AND MURPHY  IS THE BEST !

This firm was truly exceptional during one of the hardest times in my life.
My aunt, who is also a lawyer, did her research and found them to be the best — and she was absolutely right. From day one, I felt completely taken care of. I was referred to top-notch doctors and treatment providers, and every step of the process was handled with care and professionalism.

My attorneys, Lonneke and Todd, were not only kind and supportive but also fierce advocates when it came to dealing with the insurance companies and their lawyers. They spoke directly with my care team, managed every detail of my case, and consistently kept my best interests at heart. Their paralegal, Yvette, was incredibly compassionate and made me feel seen and supported — her kindness truly reflected the firm’s values and hospitality.

Despite the complexity of my case (due to pre-existing injuries), they fought tirelessly and ultimately secured the full policy limit for my settlement. They made a difficult and overwhelming process feel smooth and manageable. I always felt safe, heard, and well-informed.

It was easy to be completely honest with them, and I trusted them entirely through every challenge this car accident brought into my life. They advocated for me daily, and honestly, I was a little sad when the case came to a close — not because of the outcome (which was incredible), but because I genuinely enjoyed working with them.

They are top-tier accident attorneys — professional, knowledgeable, responsive, and truly invested in their clients. I couldn’t recommend them more highly. If you’re looking for legal representation after an accident, don’t think twice. You’ll be in the best hands possible.

Lonneke and Todd were great.  They walked me through the process and made this easy.  They explained the good and the bad and guided me to make the right decision.  I'd highly recommend their services, in fact I've already sent them a referral.

l.

The communication throughout the process was exemplary. I was kept informed at every stage. The office staff was friendly and knowledgeable, and I especially enjoyed working with Yvette.

Thank you, Restivo & Murphy for seeing me through one of the most difficult events in my life with compassion and expertise.

Brandy A. Smith, Esq.

reviews.text:
Ms. Mecklosky was very efficient and very good at her job.  I had some outstanding moving violations from several years ago, and expected it to take a month or more to clear them and possibly thousands of dollars and leave me with points on my license.  I called her on Thursday; she had them all cleared by the following Wednesday evening, and it only took that long because some of them were in Lynbrook, where cases are only heard Wednesday night.

She also pled my offences down to 4 counts of jaywalking, saving me hundreds of dollars in fines and fees (it at least made up for what I paid her for her services) and making it so that I would have no points on my license.

She did all this while never needing me to come into her office; I had paperwork to fill out, which she emailed to me; I printed it, filled it out, got one item notarized, and scanned it and emailed it back to her.

She was very professional and kept me constantly apprised of the state of my case, both by email and phone call, and also offered texting as an option.

I would highly recommend her services to anyone looking to resolve traffic tickets, especially if they're old; I will be saving her number and calling her again if I ever have any other issues.

Although I was initially hesitant when choosing legal counsel, it quickly became clear that I had made the right decision. Your reputation as a first class, highly intelligent, and approachable attorney was fully reflected in every aspect of your work.

Workers’ compensation law is an area marked by procedural complexity and persistent challenges. Your extensive knowledge of this field and your ability to navigate its intricacies with precision were instrumental in the successful resolution of my claim. You approached each phase of the case with thoughtful analysis, strategic judgment, and careful attention to detail, ensuring that every step was handled with purpose and clarity.

Equally deserving of recognition is your exceptional team. Your staff consistently demonstrated professionalism, responsiveness, and genuine care. Phil, your paralegal, was meticulous and dependable, ensuring that all documentation and communications were handled efficiently. Cassidy, Stephanie, and Vincent each contributed meaningfully through their attentiveness, organization, and support, creating a cohesive and highly effective office environment. Their collective efforts greatly enhanced my experience and confidence throughout the process.

You and your team exceeded all expectations of legal counsel. The resolution you achieved has provided both closure and a renewed sense of security, allowing me to move forward with confidence.

Please accept my deepest thanks for your dedication, professionalism, and exceptional service. I hold you and your staff in the highest regard and would not hesitate to offer my strongest recommendation to anyone seeking outstanding legal representation.

With sincere appreciation and respect,

Sincerely,
Elizabeth Chimienti

I was honestly taken aback by some of the negative reviews regarding Fanning & Fiore, as they are simply not accurate. In my experience, both attorneys Douglas Fanning and John Fiore are true professionals.

I have worked more closely with John Fiore, and he is nothing short of exceptional. He is highly knowledgeable, professional, and consistently takes the time to clearly explain every detail while addressing questions thoroughly and respectfully.

The staff at Fanning & Fiore are outstanding. I have worked directly with Phil, Cassidy, Stephanie, and Vincent, and every interaction has been prompt, professional, and supportive. This firm operates efficiently and treats its clients with genuine respect.

After reading several of the negative reviews more closely, it became clear that many were misguided and rooted in misunderstandings of the legal process rather than any failure on the part of the firm. Legal matters are complex, and frustration does not equate to incompetence.

Based on my firsthand experience, Fanning & Fiore are a serious, capable, and trustworthy law firm. I have full confidence in their work and would strongly recommend their services without hesitation.

Sincerely,
Elizabeth Chimienti

Total professionals !! I can say nothing but great things about John Fiore and his staff . Always on top of everything and John always takes my calls when he is available and if he’s not, he calls me back as soon as he can . I am so happy I fired my other firm and went with John . I have recommended him and will continue to recommend him. Both he and all of the staff are incredible and really care about what they do and who they represent!!

John has gone above and beyond what is an attorney's responsibly. Any questions I had if he didn't know the answers, he knew someone who did. I would highly recommend him to anyone who needs a compensation attorney.

I was a client of Fanning & Fiore and had two cases with them. Based on my experience, I do not recommend this law office.

At first, things seemed to be going well — my medical bills were covered and I received compensation for time I couldn’t work. I rarely contacted the office, only checking in every few months. I once called and  spoke with Phil. I asked him about my cases to which he  responded dismissively with “I already told you that,” even though I hadn’t spoken to him in months.

Later in a future call, I was shocked to learn from the office that both of my cases had been closed without my knowledge or consent. When I raised this directly with the lawyer, he told me my case was not closed and that he had seen cases remain open for years. He offered no apology for the misinformation or the unprofessional behavior of his staff.

To make matters worse, I even had an email blocked from someone in their office. Despite always treating them with respect and patience, I was met with dishonesty and disrespect. That is unacceptable and unprofessional.

I ultimately decided to change lawyers. In my opinion, Fanning & Fiore are not worth working with.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Marcello,
Thank you for your feedback. When you initially tried to reach “Brittney” via email, she had not been with our firm for several years and her email address was no longer active. Fortunately, that did not prevent you from continuing to communicate directly with us on multiple occasions afterward, and we were always available to assist you.
To clarify the facts of your case while we represented you:

You received all necessary medical treatment benefits.
You were fully compensated for your lost time from work.
Your then-attorney informed you that your case remained open (and indeed it was).

Our records also show that in June 2023, you called our office several times in one morning. On each occasion, our staff spoke with you patiently and thoroughly addressed your concerns. As we explained during those conversations, your case was still active, and we were prepared to continue fighting on your behalf had you not chosen to substitute counsel.
We are proud to have secured both medical benefits and monetary compensation for you, and we remained ready to advocate further until you decided to retain new representation.
While we respect your right to leave a review, we believe a one-star rating does not accurately reflect the outcome we achieved together or our ongoing availability to you.
We sincerely wish you continued success and good health moving forward.
Best regards,
Fanning & Fiore, LLP

reviews.text:
If you're ever in a moment of deep crisis, when everything feels like it's falling apart — Gianni is the one you want by your side. He’s not just a criminal defense attorney; he’s a real-life guardian angel. Our nightmare began at midnight. It was one of the darkest moments of our lives — terrifying, unexpected, and overwhelming. We called Gianni, not expecting anyone to pick up at that hour. We were desperate and hopeless. But he answered. He picked up the phone, heard the fear in my voice — and within 10 minutes, he was there. He didn’t hesitate. He drove to us immediately, and when he arrived, it felt like a superhero had just shown up to save us. With calmness, confidence, and razor-sharp insight, he assessed the situation, asked the right questions, and took control. In a matter of minutes, he had a plan — and more importantly, he gave us hope when we had none. His presence alone brought comfort to me and my entire family. We will never forget that night, nor the way he stood by us from the very beginning.
Gianni didn’t just “take a case.” He devoted himself to fighting for our future. He communicated with us constantly, reviewed every piece of evidence with care, and never once treated us like just another file. His determination, legal brilliance, and compassion are unmatched. What he achieved for us felt impossible — and yet he made it real. Thanks to his tireless work, the outcome of our case was better than we ever could have imagined. I can say with absolute certainty that no other attorney could have done what he did. No one else would have fought as hard, cared as much, or stood as firmly in our corner. Gianni doesn’t just work for money — he works for justice. He works for people. He fights for families, futures, and second chances. He sees his clients as human beings, and he never gives up, even when the odds are stacked high.
If you're searching for justice in Nassau County — stop searching. Gianni is the one.
From the bottom of our hearts, thank you, Gianni. We will never forget what you did for us.

He's very well spoken and he is a Lawyer who is on top of his game. It took some time, but it was worth the wait, was not disappointed at all. reviews.text: I never write reviews and I have never had to obtain legal representation of any kind.  Nonetheless, I recently had an injury at my workplace, and a colleague gave me the name of an attorney who then connected me with Gennaro Battiloro of the Battiloro Law Group.  I am on workers comp and still in the process of trying to get the treatment I need (surgery).  After 2 1/2 months the insurance co that provides WC payments for my employer just STOPPED everything and then denies my case. I immediately contacted Mr. Battiloro's office and asked his office mgr to schedule an appointment for me to meet with him and discuss my concerns over the reasons for denial and review my case. They're allegations were completely unbiased.  At our meeting we reviewed all the facts, their denial and he did some quick research while I was in his office and he immediately contacted the claims examiner and reviewed and clarified the FACTS with her.  The very next day my workers comp claim was reopened by the claims examiner and my indemnity payments were resumed.  My WC hearing was a few days after this and thank God my case was re-opened and the other injured body parts were included.  Gennaro Battiloro has been very responsive when I reach out to him with any questions, and, thank God, he had my case reopened with Insurance Carrier immediately. For a single parent and provider I so appreciate his work, professionalism, and responsiveness to my questions, concerns and moving forward with my workers comp claim. We are still in the process, and I continue to count on him and his team to get us to the finish line successfully - so I can get the surgery I need and recover from my injuries. I called him "The Magic Man", as he performed Magic overnight.  Highly recommend him to anyone needing legal representation from any Work Related Injury.  Will keep you updated with my case.
reviews.ownerResponse: Lissette, we cannot express enough how thankful we are for this wonderful review. We are truly lucky to have you as part of TBLG Community, and knowing you are happy makes us happy. We now call Gennaro "The Magic Man" and he loves it! Be well and stay healthy.

reviews.text:
I retained Peter Brill in 2022 after seeing a number of positive reviews, but my experience did not reflect that. Initially, communication was prompt and professional. However, after retaining him, communication declined significantly. Many of my calls went unanswered for weeks or months at a time, and I was often routed to an answering service until others intervened on my behalf to reach him. I contacted the Grievance Committee regarding these concerns and also because I received multiple late-night emails from Mr. Brill threatening to sue me for defamation after I wrote an honest review of my experience and provided supporting documentation. The Grievance Committee was made aware of the situation along with screenshots and other necessary documentation.

Overall, my main concern was the lack of consistent communication and the fact that the services I retained him for were not delivered as agreed. I never received any proof of correspondence between Mr. Brill and other parties advocating on my behalf, furthering negative press and inaccurate information, to which he was retained between 2022 to present.

My review is solely based on my personal experience. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to me directly.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Black is apparently not aware of the lawsuit pending against her for defamation and libel as to her previous false statements about her interactions with our firm, or she wouldn't be reposting the same lies again here, creating additional causes of action for us against her.

Let me reiterate our previous response to the fake "review" that she wisely took down previously. Natalie Black was the subject of the following article in the New York Post: "Probe finds NYC teachers sent raunchy **xts, **xually preyed on students" because the following misconduct was substantiated against her by the New York City Department of Education Special Commissioner of Investigation:

"Natalie Black, 27, a teacher at Hillside Arts and Letters Academy in Queens, sent at least 15 raunchy snaps of herself to a 17-year-old male student beginning in late 2021. The boy was interviewed by the NYPD, but no charges were filed.

By March 2022, Black’s bad behavior escalated: she showed male students photos of her va****, pulled down her pants in a student’s home and told him to “eat my a**,” and sent kids videos of herself “deep thr***ing” a liquor bottle and dancing n***d from the waist down.

Black refused to cooperate with SCI investigators. She was “removed from DOE service” while the probe was ongoing, the report noted.

The SCI report recommended that Black be permanently removed from the DOE workforce, “given her total unfitness as a pedagogue.”

https://nypost.com/2022/10/08/female-nyc-teachers-were-equal-opportunity-predators-probe/

Ms. Black believed that she should be provided with services without paying for them. She paid a minimal retainer that covered three hours of work and demanded that we continue ro represent her without charging her for additional time. We did actually provide her some additional work without additional charge, but when we told her we could not continue without charging her, she became abusive and threatened to post negative reviews. We are now suing her.

reviews.text: My best friend was facing decades in prison after being charged with three armed robberies in Nassau County.  This all happened over a year ago when he was deep into his addiction.  Attorney Brill actually knew the prosecutor who was handling the case and he was able to make contact with them very early on in the process.  He also knew the lead investigator who deals with major armed robberies in Nassau.  My friend really did fall off of the deep end, and he got himself into a huge mess.  Everyone is thankful that Peter was able to make some concessions with the prosecutor since there were no previous convictions.  What saved him was that I did not use a real gun during the commission of the robberies.  After nearly 18 months and several delays my friend ended-up being convicted of just a single conviction with a suspended sentence and probation.   His family and mine appreciate the expertise of Peter Brill.

reviews.text:
Sullivan&Brill– Medical Malpractice Case

It’s hard to put into words how much Joseph and Steven  and the entire team at Sullivan & Brill attorneys mean to our family. For 11 long years, they stood by us through one of the most painful and challenging times of our lives — fighting for justice in my father’s medical malpractice case.

What happened to my father changed our lives forever. We knew the road ahead would be long, but we never expected it to take over a decade. Through it all, Joe and Steve never gave up. They were patient, persistent, and incredibly compassionate — treating us not just like clients, but like people who mattered.

There were times when the process felt overwhelming, but their commitment never wavered. They kept us informed, fought tirelessly on our behalf, and gave us hope when it was hard to find any. After 11 years, we finally won the case — and it meant everything to finally hear those words.

We are forever grateful for their dedication, strength, and heart. If you’re looking for a legal team that will truly fight for you and never give up — no matter how long it takes — I cannot recommend Sullivan & Brill attorneys enough.

Thank you, from the bottom of our hearts.

— Jose Dominguez
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Jose. Your father’s case was a complicated and hard fought medical malpractice and civil rights case. It gives us great satisfaction to know that we have made such a positive impact on your family.

reviews.text: I have nothing but amazing things to say about Arn (yes he told me to call him Arn).  He helped, AFTER 6 YEARS, to get my mortgage company to provide me with a modification. Well, onto my 4th lawyer (Arn Bottalico) and he told me something different.  My case was an extremely difficult one (I won't get into details).  What Arn DID guaranty was that he would give it everything he had.  But could not guaranty a positive outcome.  As scary as that notion was it was still refreshing to hear.  He laid out everything I had going for me and everything I had going against me.  And after several months......FINALLY.......I have my modification.  His rates are more than reasonable and he worked with me (and continues to work with me).  My only regret is not having found him 6 years ago.  What more can I say?  He is a very kind and gentle soul.  He will not make you feel pressured into doing anything.  He will provide you with options and allow you to make your choice.  His demeanor makes you want to trust him and believe in him.  And I did.  And it turned out amazingly.  My family and I are indebted to Arn.  Thank you for all your efforts, Arn. My friend began the process of filing the initial paperwork for divorce with the court but understood that if his wife hired an attorney, then he would need proper representation.  After serving his wife, she found an attorney who seem to move quickly towards resolving the divorce.  My friend needed assistance with legal representation and asked me to help him find an attorney to assist with the divorce.  As I researched for divorce attorneys, I was fortunate to have been referred to Attorney Gillian Lewter.  The person highly recommending Attorney Lewter explained that if he hired her, my friend would have the best representation.  After sharing the recommendation with my friend, I was asked to contact her on his behalf.  Based upon the initial consultation with Attorney Lewter, I am pleased to share that Attorney Lewter exceeding my expectations.  Attorney Lewter is kind but stern and well versed in this area of law.  My friend arranged to consult with Attorney Lewter which put him at ease knowing that he retained the best person to handle his divorce.  Although Attorney Lewter explained various ways this case could proceed, her goal was to settle this matter quickly and in the most cost- effective manner.
During negotiations, Attorney Lewter stood firm on behalf of my friend.  For every erroneous accusation made by opposing counsel’s client, Attorney Lewter focused on issues that were relevant to the case.  At the end of this journey, my friend was thankful and expressed to me; “My attorney did a wonderful job.  I am satisfied with the way she handled my case and my divorce settlement.”
Thank you for your brilliant approach in helping my friend.  I appreciate and respect your experience and most importantly your candor.  Thank you for demonstrating why it is necessary to have a knowledgeable attorney fight for you during complicated divorces such as this.  My friend and I strongly recommend you and your amazing team at Lewter Law Firm, P.C.  May you continue to have great success and be blessed with a practice that grows well beyond your vision.

reviews.text:
Would never recommend this lawyer. I really don’t understand how others do when all she does is overcharge her clients. She rarely returns calls and has unreasonable fees. For example, a simple two-second phone call reminding you of a court date—something you didn’t even request—costs $50. An email is $150, and sending an email on your behalf is $250. Her hourly rate is $600, and she charges $600 for travel and $60 to $100 for parking. To review her own file before going to court costs $400. The basic things for any lawyer to do is meet with their client before any hearing she does not even though she did not see or hear from the person for over 6 months. How can you speak for that person? And turn around and charge the person $4500 for that 30-minute court hearing? If that is not a scam, I really do not know what is.

For her to show up for a hearing, it could cost you between $3,000 and $4,000. She seems indifferent to her clients, focusing only on dragging out cases. Whenever the opposing side makes requests, she does not advocate for her clients; she allows things to drag on unnecessarily.

A friend of mine hired her, and she completely ruined them. They had to file for bankruptcy, and the other side won everything. She lied to them throughout the hearing and essentially sold them out to the opposing side. If you are currently represented by her, I urge you to leave; it will not get better, and you may face serious consequences.

Her retainer fee is $5,000, which quickly disappears in charges. It's a shame to see how the divorce court allows people like her to exploit vulnerable individuals. She was even late for court once, and when the person called to check on her arrival, they were charged $160 for that brief phone call. A case that should have taken three months to a year took over four years. For a trial, she charges over $12000 for just that one day. Ultimately, the individual lost everything and is now homeless. I will go on every platform and expose this person. She should not be allowed to scam and mislead people like that.

reviews.text: I had a consultation with Mr Levy. He was so wonderful- smart, compassionate, considerate. He spent a lot of time with me. I have met several attorneys, and this firm is far beyond the others. I decided to retain them, and I have no doubt that I am in good hands.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Laurie,

Thank you so much for taking the time to share this, it truly means the world to us. We know that reaching out to a new attorney, especially after a difficult experience with prior counsel, takes real courage. We want you to know that your trust is something we take very seriously and do not take for granted.

Me and our entire team believe that every client deserves to feel heard, respected, and fiercely advocated for, not just in the courtroom (or waiting to communicate with clients only close to date of appearances), but from the very first conversation. We understand that divorce is one of the most personal and emotionally demanding experiences a person can go through, and you deserve an attorney who understands not just the legal landscape, but your story, your goals, your family, and what matters most to you.

That is exactly where we begin. Before strategy, before paperwork, before anything else, WE LISTEN! We take the time to truly get to know you, the full context of your situation, and what a successful outcome looks like for your life going forward. You came to us having not felt fully protected, and we are committed to changing that.

You are in good hands. We look forward to standing in your corner every step of the way. Thank you for your kinds words and review.

reviews.text: Amazing attorneys, Amazing people- This is who you want to call; Highly recommended!

Jason,

Helping a father fight for his daughter is one of the most meaningful things we get to do. Behind every case is a real family, and we never lose sight of that. It was an absolute honor to help you.

We wish you both a lifetime of beautiful moments together. And if you ever need us again, we’re always just a phone call away. Thank you for the kind words and review.

Fortuna,

Thank you so much for your incredibly kind words. It truly means a great deal to me.

I know this divorce was one of the most painful and overwhelming chapters of your life, and from the very beginning, I understood how much was at stake for you and your family. This was not just about ending a marriage. It involved your children, custody, child support, maintenance, businesses, property, and the very real concern that assets were being hidden. Those are heavy burdens for any mother and wife to carry, and I never lost sight of how personal and important this was for you.

You showed tremendous strength, patience, and courage throughout the process. Even when things felt uncertain, you stayed committed to protecting your children and securing your future. I could not have achieved such a favorable outcome without your trust, your resolve, and your willingness to keep moving forward through an extremely difficult situation.

It was an honor to stand beside you, guide you, and fight for you every step of the way. On behalf of myself and my team, thank you for placing your confidence in us during such a critical time in your life. We are truly grateful, and we wish you and your children peace, stability, and a brighter new beginning. Thank you for your kind words and review.

Nadeen,

Reading your kind words truly made my day, and I want you to know how much this means to me, both personally and professionally.

When I chose this profession, it was with one guiding principle, that every person who walks through my door deserves to feel heard, respected, and genuinely supported. Knowing that you've felt that level of care and attention means I'm living up to that promise, and that is the greatest reward I could ask for.

Your trust is something I never take lightly. Behind every question you ask is a real concern, a real worry, and a real life, and I want you to know that I see that every single time we speak. Making sure you feel informed and confident in every step of this process isn't just part of my job; it's something I'm deeply committed to on a personal level.

I also want to take a moment to thank you. Working with clients who are open, communicative, and engaged makes such a meaningful difference. Your willingness to ask questions and stay involved in the process is a big part of why our experience together has been so positive.

We still have work ahead of us, and I want you to know that my dedication to your matter and to you as a person will never waver. I'm here for you every step of the way.

From the bottom of my heart, thank you for your trust, your confidence, and for taking the time to share such generous words. It truly means the world to me. Thank you for your kind words and review.

reviews.text: I consulted with Itamar Levy regarding a Family Court matter and was very impressed with his professionalism, knowledge, and responsiveness. Although I ultimately chose to resolve the matter outside of court and did not retain him, I truly appreciated his guidance. reviews.ownerResponse:
Darwin,

Thank you so much for your thoughtful words. It truly means a great deal.

Family Court matters are deeply personal, and I’m glad you felt supported and informed during our consultation and legal guidance. Even when someone ultimately resolves things outside of court, being able to provide clarity and direction during a stressful time is something I take very seriously.

We have the privilege of representing many members of the NYPD and other law enforcement officers, as well as first responders throughout New York. These are individuals who dedicate their lives to protecting and serving our communities. It is always an honor to stand beside them when they need someone in their corner. The same commitment, discipline, and integrity they bring to their work is what we strive to bring to theirs.

Supporting those who protect others is not something we take lightly. We understand the unique pressures, schedules, and sensitivities that can come with these careers, especially when family matters arise. Providing steady guidance, discretion, and strong advocacy is the least we can do in return for the service they provide every day.

I truly appreciate your trust and your recommendation. If you ever need anything in the future, I will be here for you. Thank you for taking the time to leave a review and your kind words.

reviews.text:

reviews.ownerResponse:
Evelyn,

Thank you so much for taking the time to share this.

Custody matters are deeply personal, and we understand how emotional and overwhelming they can feel. When a parent reaches out to us, our first priority is to truly listen and to understand the full picture before offering guidance. You deserve to feel heard, respected, and supported from the very first conversation.

There is no “one-size-fits-all” approach when it comes to children. Every family dynamic is different, and the legal strategy must reflect that. We take great care to explain the process clearly, outline realistic options, and help you make informed decisions that protect both you and your child.

It means a great deal to know that you felt comfortable and supported during our consultation. Helping parents navigate these challenging moments with clarity and compassion is exactly why we do what we do.

We truly appreciate your trust and recommendation. Thank you for the review and your kind words

reviews.text: I had an amazing consultation with Itamar. reviews.ownerResponse:
Jennifer,

Thank you so much for your incredibly thoughtful words. It truly means a great deal.

Family Court matters, especially when a mother is trying to protect, support, and advocate for her children, are deeply personal and emotionally layered. These are not just legal cases; they involve your family, your future, and most importantly, your children. We never lose sight of that.

Our approach is always client focused because every family’s situation is unique. There is no template solution when it comes to parenting issues, custody, or support. We take the time to listen carefully, understand your specific concerns, and develop a strategy that reflects your goals and your child’s best interests.

As a parent myself to a 10 year old daughter and 7 year old son, I understand firsthand how paramount children are in every decision. Their stability, safety, and emotional well-being must always come first. That perspective isn’t just professional, it’s personal. We genuinely care about protecting the parent child relationship and guiding families through the court process in a way that prioritizes what truly matters.

Thank you again for placing your trust in us. We look forward to standing beside you and helping you navigate this chapter with clarity, strength, and confidence. Thank you for your kind words and review.

reviews.text: Itamar Levy is very professional and took the time to understand my case before taking it on!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ashley,

Thank you for your thoughtful words. We truly appreciate you taking the time to share your experience.

Family law matters are sensitive, serious, and often deeply personal. The decisions made in these cases can have lasting effects on finances, parenting arrangements, and family relationships. That’s why we believe it’s critical to fully understand the facts, the dynamics, and the long term implications before moving forward.

At our firm, we are intentional about evaluating whether and how we can truly help before asking a client to invest additional time or money. Just as importantly, we are always mindful not to take any step that could unintentionally place a client, or their relationship with their children in a more difficult position.

These situations deserve care, strategy, and thoughtful guidance. We’re grateful for the trust you placed in us and are honored to stand beside our clients during such important moments in their lives. Thank you for your kind words and review.

reviews.text: I spoke with Itamar Levy directly about navigating a challenging custodial situation. He was responsive, direct, and honest. I’m not super comfortable speaking with lawyers, but Itamar was a breeze during our consultation. Not to mention, immensely knowledgeable and humanistic. Highly recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Alyssa,

Thank you so much for your thoughtful words. It truly means a great deal.

Many people come into a consultation feeling anxious or guarded. There’s often a belief that speaking to a lawyer will feel intimidating, cold, or purely transactional. Family law matters are deeply personal, and opening up about them can feel overwhelming. We understand that.

At our firm, creating a space that feels safe, respectful, and non-judgmental is not an afterthought, it’s foundational to how we practice. Challenging custody issues and other family law matters are already heavy. The last thing anyone needs is to feel uncomfortable asking questions or expressing concerns.

There is also a lot of information circulating, from friends, online searches, forums, AI tools, and social media. While some of it can be helpful, much of it lacks context or doesn’t apply to a specific situation, and it not helpful. Part of our role is to help separate noise from reality, clarify misconceptions, and provide guidance that is grounded in experience and the law as it actually works.

We’re here to educate, to advocate, and to support our clients. That means being direct and honest, but also compassionate and human. Legal situations can feel isolating. Our goal is to make sure our clients never feel like they’re navigating them alone.

We appreciate your trust and your recommendation more than you know. Thank you for your kind words and review.

Itamar Levy, Esq. - Always here to help 😉

Jay,

When someone contacts our firm, it’s often during a time filled with uncertainty, stress, and important decisions about their family and future. We understand how much is at stake. From the very first conversation, our goal is to provide clarity, confidence, and a steady hand to guide you forward.

Family law matters are deeply personal. They affect parents, children, and entire households. It is genuinely our privilege to guide you through this chapter with clarity, compassion, and strength. We are committed to protecting what matters most to you and helping you move forward with confidence and peace of mind. Thank you for your review and let us know if you need anything else.

reviews.text: Very nice and extremely helpful. If you want answers, they shoot straight. Willing to answer any and all questions. Free consultation was extremely knowledgeable. Definitely feel comfortable
reviews.ownerResponse:
Janet,

Thank you so much for your thoughtful words. They truly mean a great deal.

Going through a divorce is never easy. It’s emotional, personal, and often overwhelming. When someone trusts us to guide them through that chapter of their life, we don’t take that lightly. We understand that behind every case file is a real person navigating uncertainty, stress, and major life changes.

We believe in treating every client the way we would want to be treated ourselves, with honesty, responsiveness, respect, and compassion. That means giving straight answers, even when they’re difficult. It means being available to answer questions. It means taking the time to explain the process so you feel informed and empowered, not confused or left in the dark.

What truly differentiates us is that we care. Not just about the outcome, but about the person sitting across from us. We fight hard when needed, but we also listen. We strategize, but we also support. Divorce is a legal process, but for our clients, it’s life. And we approach it that way.

reviews.text: Janet, Alexa & Lori at JCS Elder Law are wonderful ladies.  They offered support and guidance in making important decisions with estate planning.  They take the time to answer questions and explain things completely.  The comfort and support offered throughout the process made me feel confident in the decisions my husband and I had made.  The ladies even came to my ill Mom’s residence to help ease the process.  We are very grateful and thankful for Janet, Alexa & Lori and the services they have provided to our family.
Janet is an extremely kind and welcoming person. Both her and Lori are very pleasant to work with.

Working with JCS Elder Law was phenomonal. From the moment we walked through the door, we were met with warmth, patience, and a level of professionalism that immediately put us at ease.

What truly stood out was how incredibly knowledgeable the entire team was. Estate planning can be daunting, complicated and overwhelming, but they navigated every detail with absolute mastery. Their depth of understanding was clear in every conversation—whether explaining nuanced legal options, answering our many questions, or anticipating needs we hadn’t even considered. No stone was left unturned, and no question was ever too small.

Beyond their legal expertise, the way they treated my elderly parents was nothing short of extraordinary. They were patient, respectful, and deeply considerate. It wasn’t just about documents and legalities—it was about people, families, and peace of mind.

The combination of razor-sharp legal skill and heartfelt compassion is rare, and JCS Elder Law brings both in abundance. I fully recommend JCS Elder Law without hesitation to anyone looking for estate planning services done with integrity, intelligence, and true care.

Unbelievably dedicated team.  Extremely knowledgeable and in my case, persistent and willing to go to bat for their client.  While it’s never a good sign when you need this kind of help, I’m truly thankful I called them.

Janet is welcoming and compassionate in an often-difficult field, where the sensitivity of her clients and their families is absolutely paramount. I have witnessed more times than I can count how her expertise and dedication has resulted in successful outcomes for her clients, many of which are life-changing sources of relief for them and their families. Her team is organized and responsive in situations where there often countless moving parts. Their professionalism and dedication are admirable.

Like many people, I put off making a will for decades. It’s difficult to think about one’s mortality, and even more overwhelming to consider all of the decisions that will need to be made to maintain your health and financial well-being when you are no longer able. And who can we trust to help us put our final wishes into legal documents? I was extremely fortunate to find Janet Cohen Sorrentino and her team at JCS Elder Law. Janet took exquisite care and time to explain my choices, and the pros and cons of each. She has an impressive gift for translating legalese into lay person’s language. Using real life examples, she illustrated various scenarios that “could” happen, ways in which they would impact my estate, and strategies for assuring that my wishes would be carried out. Janet’s approach was always kind, yet she had a knack for injecting humor just when it was needed. She made this heavy task so much easier.

Taking a holistic approach, she also made sure I updated my living will, health care proxy, and power of attorney, and explained the nuances and impact of each choice. Janet’s breadth of professional and personal experience, along with her rock solid moral and ethical values, and fierce sense of advocacy, reassured me that she always had my interests at the forefront of every decision.

Janet’s team members are a reflection of her values and work ethic. My interactions with Lori and Alexa were professional, efficient, and, like those with Janet, kind. Together, they worked with me to finalize all of my paperwork. I now have a will and all of the documents I need to allow me to live my final years with peace of mind. Thank you, Janet and team!

I was and still am in a very difficult situation with my family. Choosing JCS Elder law had been one of the best decisions I have made. Janet , Alexa , and Laurie have put me on a successful path. Their guidance through a most difficult time has been invaluable to me and my family. Their commitment and compassion to our needs has been nothing short of a miracle. Though our road continues I am confident with the choice I made by choosing these amazing caring individuals. I couldn’t have gotten to this point in my life without their commitment to my family.
Layne Plompen ❤️

Janet and her staff have helped my family and many friends and clients as well. She made a hard time in their lives much easier. Her bedside manner is like no other attorney I have ever encountered. When you sit with her and her staff, you really feel like you are being cared for and looked after. She has her clients' best interests in mind above all and frankly is not charging enough money for what she does.

Unethical! Understaffed! Does not look out for best interest of aging client. Treats family members poorly who have best interest of  parent with nothing to gain and lawyer sides with disgruntled sister who has relationship with firm. Poor judgment. Save your money and go to a reputable elder care lawyer. Does not return phone calls or e-mails. Spent $5,000 for nothing.
reviews.ownerResponse: I believe you mistook my explanation - that, as an attorney, I cannot take direction for, nor discuss the details of another person's Last Will and Testament. It's unequivocally unethical. I worked with your mother and successfully obtained round the clock personal care aide services, supplies, case management for her at home while preserving her assets and monthly income - during a pandemic, no less! Although I tried to listen to you with sensitivity and respect, I simply could not draft a Will to remove another family member as a beneficiary unless your mother - my client - expressed that to me directly, privately and before two witnesses. I am sorry that you perceived this explanation as an attack or judgement of your motives. It was not. Regarding returning calls, we pride ourselves on returning calls within 24 hours, nearly without fail. However, ********, you are not our client - your mother was! Our ethical and professional obligations were to her. We understand that you were trying to assist your mother and facilitate legal services. I hope you can come to understand the challenging dilemma we face as Elder Law attorneys.

reviews.text:
I want to express my heartfelt gratitude for the exceptional legal assistance we received from the Russo Law Group. Our situation was, to put it mildly, dire. We were facing an imminent and critical deadline for the renewal of our essential medical coverage, simultaneously entangled in a bewildering and deeply concerning internal records discrepancy.

Honestly, the prospect of navigating the legal system felt overwhelming. However, from our very first contact, we were met with a sense of genuine care and professionalism that immediately began to alleviate our concerns. Cindy Abrehu provided us with a truly welcoming and reassuring introduction, swiftly arranging our initial consultation.

It was during this critical juncture that we had the immense privilege of meeting our attorney Kim Christian. Ms. Christian demonstrated an extraordinary level of dedication that continued relentlessly throughout the Christmas holiday season, working late evenings and weekends.

Ms. Christian's professionalism, dedication, and strategic acumen were nothing short of remarkable. There were moments when digital limitations presented significant hurdles, requiring her to painstakingly re-process vast files, breaking them down into manageable batches, all while ensuring every exhibit was perfectly assembled and accounted for. She met every unexpected obstacle, every 'bump in the road,' with an uncompromising resolve and a brilliant problem-solving approach.

The successful outcome – the granting of our medical coverage – is a direct testament to her tireless efforts and unwavering commitment. When people speak of someone going "the extra mile," I can unequivocally state that Ms. Christian consistently went an extra-extra mile for us, demonstrating a level of advocacy that truly transformed our predicament.

We are deeply grateful for the invaluable support and expert guidance provided by Ms. Christian and the entire team of the Russo Law Group.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you, Natasha, for taking the time to share such a detailed and thoughtful review. We’re so glad we could assist you during what sounds like an incredibly stressful situation. Cindy and Kim’s dedication and hard work are exactly what we aim to provide for every client, and it’s rewarding to know their efforts made a meaningful impact.

Your feedback reinforces the importance of the work we do, and we’re grateful for the opportunity to have helped you achieve a successful outcome. Thank you again for your kind words.

reviews.text:
Attorney Diana Lattanzio handled my grandmother’s will. Following her passing, our family appeared in person solely to ask whether the firm had possession of the original will. No legal advice or confidential information was requested.

Ms. Lattanzio refused to communicate entirely, did not acknowledge our presence, did not come out of her office, and offered no condolences. We were dismissed without explanation. This interaction was documented, including the date, individuals present, and the conduct observed.

While we understand that substantive communication may be limited to the executor, professional courtesy, basic acknowledgment, and respectful treatment of grieving family members are not prohibited by ethical rules. The secretary’s condescending and dismissive demeanor further compounded the issue.

Given the nature of this interaction, we are formally reviewing our options, including submission of a complaint to the appropriate attorney grievance and professional oversight authorities. This review is posted to accurately reflect our experience and to invite accountability and response.

We met with Katie Ann Trotta to do our Estate Planning and Will.  She reviewed our. assets and goals.  Ms Trotta was very helpful. She is Professional Knowledgeable and soft spoken.  After she completed she went over every step so we could discuss and understand. Her fees are reasonable.  It’s easy to get in touch with Ms. Trotta.  We recommend her to anyone interested in getting a good Estate Planning and will.  She updated our CPA.  She made conference calls.

reviews.text:
DO NOT HIRE Russo Law Group for Property Disputes
If you're looking for lawyers who will avoid going to court at all costs, lack aggression, and seem more interested in running up your bill than actually fighting for you, look no further than Russo Law Group. This firm has no teeth, no fight, and will do absolutely nothing to protect your interests.
From the very beginning, they failed to mention that they don’t even go to court. Despite the ongoing and obvious issues with my co-owner—my brother—they strung me along for over two years, charging $5,000 upfront, another $9,000 later, and then hitting me with an additional $27,500 at closing without any prior disclosure. All this, and yet no real progress was made.
They allowed my brother’s lawyer—a local storefront real estate attorney charging only $4,400 over two years—to completely push them around, dictate the terms, and walk all over me. My lawyer had no fight in them to stand up to the other lawyer, protect my interests, or stop me from being taken advantage of. My brother unilaterally evicted tenants, costing me 18 months of rental income, and I made it clear I would not pay for renovations and wanted the property sold as-is. Despite this, Russo Law Group let my brother take full control of the situation while I was left with no say.
To make matters worse, after all that time and money, they revealed that they don’t handle court cases and would simply pass me off to another firm for legal action. Why wasn’t this disclosed upfront? Adding insult to injury, my original lawyer left the firm, and I was reassigned to someone completely unfamiliar with my case.

Russo Law Group is toothless, ineffective, and a complete waste of time and money. They failed to protect my rights, allowed the other side to steamroll me, and drained me financially while leaving me worse off than when I started. If you need a lawyer to actually fight for you, avoid this firm at all costs.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that you are dissatisfied with your experience, and we take concerns like yours seriously. Our goal is always to provide thoughtful, strategic, and transparent legal guidance tailored to each client’s situation.

Because every matter is unique and often involves complex legal and practical considerations, outcomes and approaches can vary. We strive to communicate clearly about available options, anticipated costs, and potential next steps throughout the representation.

We regret that you feel your expectations were not met and would welcome the opportunity to discuss your concerns directly and review the specifics of your case. Please feel free to contact our office so we may better understand your experience and address any outstanding issues.

I was fortunate to retain Diana Lattanzio, Esq., an attorney with broad experience in estate planning and Special Needs Trusts. Over the course of 6 months, Diana’s suggestions, advice and counsel were invaluable in crafting all the necessary documents and protecting my family and testamentary wishes.  She is exceedingly personable, bright and helpful. I thoroughly enjoyed working with her and gladly recommend her with the highest rating. I was formerly a judge and practicing attorney and she impressed me as one of the best of a handful of attorneys with whom I had worked with over the course of 40 plus years. reviews.text:
Posted for Anna Hannan:
My family suffered a sudden illness of my mother and she is now a long term nursing home resident. We don't have the means to pay out of pocket and on top of that I  have an adult disabled brother that will need to be protected, yet in spite of all that, discussing future plans in my family was not anything that was considered necessary. Well, as it happens, the decisions were made for us by circumstance and we needed a lawyer to sort through this and help us in a hurry.
Enter Russo Law Group, specifically  Eric J. Einhart. Once we explained our situation, not a moment was wasted and Eric hit the ground running. He met with us (Zoom meeting initially) and was able to identify our needs for full advance directives, Power of Attorney, and setting up trusts for my brother's future protection. Within a week he personally came to the Nursing Home to finalize the initial phase of our multiple needs. I am continuing to work with Eric and case manger Kathleen DeGraff. They have both been very professional and a calming presence. Their immediate knowledge of what to do and the order to do it, whereas we have multiple problems that need to be addressed at the same time instilled a large dose of confidence that we needed to think more clearly.
I would highly recommend this firm and Eric and Kathleen for any needs you may have.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with Russo Law Group. We are glad to hear that Eric J. Einhart and Kathleen DeGraff were able to provide you with the necessary support and guidance during this challenging time. I asked if there was any risk with trying for the dismissal and it failing - would I not be able to plea it down after? This would not be the case at all.  It was basically zero risk to me. We were so grateful we read Jacoby and Jacoby reviews because every one of them were real honest reviews. As soon as we walked in the door we knew we made the right decision. The girl in the office told us what to bring to the appointment very easy just bills pay stubs ID etc. when we got to the appointment he filled out a couple of forms and we saw Rich Jacoby. He explained the process the fee was very reasonable and within 2 days they called him to sign all the documents. From that point within 6 weeks he had a virtual court hearing in the Jacoby Medford office and everything was done. What a relief for him and us. Because no matter how hard he worked he couldn’t get ahead. If you need a great attorney and staff Jacoby and Jacoby is definitely for you. We will always be thankful to them.

reviews.text:
HIGHEST recommendation!

A stunningly concentrated telephone consultation from Mr. SIEGEL for a landlord/tenant dispute involving a threat of eviction via unilateral termination of my lease.

I began by telling Mr. Siegel that I have no income except Social Security, a fact which ended the discussion with other attorneys' offices I had previously contacted.

Instead of rebuffing me, Mr. Siegel listened to each and every aspect of my case,  and offered his opinion as to possible outcomes and other free legal services I might reach out to. Although his surmise in my case offered little that a resolution in my favor might happen,  even before a jury, it was great relief "to have the cards on the table" so to speak, and thoroughly explained to me by an expert!

I did promise Mr. Siegel that I would write this commendation,  in thanks for his 'paying it forward' so generously.

P. Waters

Bradley Siegel, ESQ, and his staff, did an excellent job in helping me evict my four tenants.
The process took a little over four months.
The fees were reasonable.
In that time Mr. Siegel me with me and we developed a strategy to get them out.
Together we executed the plan. Bradley’s meticulous preparation, and steadfast commitment to pursuing justice have yielded a remarkable outcome.
One tenant was not reasonable, and I had to take him to court. After 3 posponements, the judge told the tenant that he should take my offer to leave, and he finally did.
The other 3 tenants decided to take financial settlements, signing the proper documentation, and turned over their keys.
Robert Dreyfuss, CPA

I posted on LegalMatch looking to hire an attorney for a shareholder/co-op board dispute and the office of Bradley Siegel responded almost right away by email asking for more information. The following business day his office sent me a text message from a number that is set up to receive text messages and then called me shortly after to set up a phone/virtual consultation for me for the following day. I cancelled something to make time for this appointment as it was more important to me and the attorney did not call at the promised appointment time. About 10 minutes after the promised appointment time I sent a text message asking if we were still on for the consultation and received no response. I would never do business with someone who doesn’t keep their appointments and doesn’t even have the decency to call or text to say they need to cancel/reschedule. That tells me a lot about how he would’ve handled my case had I hired him. Very unprofessional!

EDIT: Since I’ve left this review, different people from this office have called me at least once a week asking me if I still need an attorney even though I have removed the LegalMatch posting. Every time I tell them that I’ve already got one but they still call me again. Extremely annoying! Today they called again telling me they saw my review and wanted to talk to me about it. No thanks, that ship has sailed! My review stands as it is.
reviews.ownerResponse: Elena- we hired a  new assistant who mistakenly scheduled the call without asking me. They also were learning the new texting system. It goes to a computer not to a phone. Sorry for any inconvenience it caused you. If you called an attorney in the office would have given you the Free consultation. By the way we offer a Free consultation and many firms don't do that.  Thanks for your understanding. I'm sure that you never made a mistakes when first starting a new job.  You probably never make mistakes.

Naushad, I’m sorry to hear about your experience. We do have in person consultations, however we need to get some basic information about your situation so we can have the appropriate person meet with you.

We need to have the location of your job (NYC, Long Island, Westchester, Upstate NY, NJ, PA, FL) and the type of discrimination (race, gender, pregnancy, age, etc.) you are experiencing.

Once we have this information, we can arrange an in person meeting with the attorney handling that location and type of discrimination.

Please feel free to call me back personally at 212-248-7431. Bryan Acre.

Update 1: In my opinion, the primary service I received was being advised to save money in an account. reviews.ownerResponse:
It is sad to see you update your review with more false claims, especially after we showed you proof that your first claims were wrong. To be completely clear with anyone reading this, we need to correct your new statements:

1. Contacting your creditor: We already sent you the emails and screenshots proving we submitted your Power of Attorney and talked to your creditors for you. We warn all clients that funders often lie and say we didn't call, just to cause trouble. We gave you the hard proof that we did.

2. The 'Fine Print' and Fees: Our starting fee of $5,000 is not a 'random high amount.' It is our normal fee. We talk about it in our very first meeting, and it is clearly written in three different parts of our contract.

3. Payment Methods: We gladly take normal payments. Our contract clearly states, "ACH is required for future payments..." to keep things running smoothly - not because we 'refuse' real money.

4. Negotiation Strategy: To resolve a debt, you must have money to offer. We kept reminding you to let us know when you had money saved. Without money to offer, we cannot make a deal, and we cannot pay your debt for you. We did exactly what you hired us to do: we handled the creditors for you until you had money to make a deal.

5. Legal Action: Suing for unpaid fees is always our absolute last choice! We only do it when someone ignores us for months and avoids paying. We are always willing to set up payment plans with clients who actually work with us, which is why we ultimately allowed our collection attorney to give you a very flexible payment plan spread out over a few years.

We stand by our work, our honesty, and the proof we gave you showing we did everything we could based on the money you had.

We still wish you all the best in your future endeavors.

After countless dead ends and only finding companies that charged hundreds or thousands of dollars with no guarantees, I finally came across The Law Offices of Kenneth H. Dramer PC. Even after paying their fee plus the settlement, it was still far less than the original balance. Best of all, I no longer have to pay maintenance fees and I avoided a negative mark on my credit.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your feedback. We'd like to offer some clarification for others who may read your review.

You contacted our office by phone with questions about a real estate matter. While we did our best to provide general guidance, we politely explained that we don’t provide legal advice over the phone. For real estate matters in particular, we invite individuals to visit our office and bring any relevant documents so we can properly review the situation and discuss how we may assist. This is our standard approach for anyone seriously considering legal representation in a real estate case.

It’s also important to note that you are not a client of our firm and did not retain us for any legal services. Our hundreds of five-star reviews come from real clients who have worked directly with our team and experienced the level of professionalism, care, and results we consistently deliver.

We sincerely wish you the best in resolving your matter.

DRAMER LEGAL GROUP & TIME SHARE EXITING

One must be paranoid these days when it comes to seeking services on the internet.

We never know whether the service will be of good quality or whether we will be getting ripped off.  We must now continually be worried about being scammed.

When it comes to exiting a timeshare, most companies require exorbitant upfront fees and monthly costs without guarantees.  One company presentation I attended wanted $3500 upfront and a total of $35,000. Time-share exit companies are now rivaling the scam of the time share companies themselves.

Dramer Legal is the real deal.

Dramer, and their representative, Michael Sininsky, made it happen and kept promises, with absolutely no fees until success.  And the fee was incredibly reasonable, only one time, with no gimmicks or extras.  They even provided the option of a monthly payment plan.

There team was responsive and communicative at each point in time.

Don’t expect an overnight fix….the process takes approximately 6 months, but the job gets done while you have already stopped time-share monthly mortgage payments and annual dues.  It was so remarkable and so helpful.

I highly recommend their services and counseling!

I can’t thank Justin Liberman at Terry Katz & Associates enough for the incredible job he did handling my Workers' Compensation case. From day one, he was responsive, compassionate, and completely committed to helping me get the care and support I needed after my injury.
One of the biggest hurdles we faced was getting my physical therapy approved. There was a lot of back-and-forth between the state and federal government over who was responsible for covering my treatment, and it honestly felt overwhelming. Justin stepped in and took control of the situation, navigating all the red tape and fighting to make sure I wasn’t left in limbo.
He kept me informed every step of the way and made sure my rights were protected throughout the process. Thanks to his hard work and persistence, my physical therapy was finally approved, and I received the compensation I was entitled to.
If you’re dealing with a complicated Workers’ Comp case—especially one with jurisdiction issues or delays in medical approval—I highly recommend Justin Liberman and the team at Terry Katz & Associates. They know the system inside and out and will fight for you every step of the way.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jason, thank you for your review! We’re glad Justin was able to guide you through the challenges of your Workers’ Compensation case, especially navigating the complex approval process for your physical therapy. Thank you for trusting us with your claim.
